Careerspan isn't a replacement... it's the 'missing layer' that makes their products defensible. 'We're not here to replace your product. We're the missing layer that makes your product defensible in the long run.'
Handshake has 1,600 universities but no way to capture behavioral signal at scale. Careerspan has the coaching infrastructure... 'Real angle is data supremacy.'
Extends data-as-ore thesis to specific arbitrage play - combining distribution + signal infrastructure

Positioning Careerspan as a strategic hedge for Handshake's declining core business ($200M, only 6% growth). 'Even if partnership never becomes major revenue line, it provides data supremacy and option value.'
Careerspan is playing a relationship-heavy, founder-to-founder game rather than a traditional enterprise sales motion... 'the who you know is more valuable than what you know'

Careerspan thesis: behavioral signal (how people think) is more valuable than profile data. 'Access without signal is noise. Employers have 20 million profiles with no way to identify who will succeed.'
Emphasizing the 3-person team as an asset (low acquisition overhead). 'We're a three-person team. We're not even that expensive to bring on.'
Part of broader acquihire positioning strategy
The real value isn't in helping someone get one job—it's in tracking their entire career trajectory and predicting future success. This creates a dataset that compounds in value over time.
